result:
## Summary

The site had pivoted products (InboxZap, an email-cleanup tool → Lumina, an AI meeting assistant) but the SEO/technical layer never caught up. Fixed:

1. **Title/meta mismatch** (`app/layout.tsx`) — was still branded "InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox," while every visible page is about Lumina. Rewrote title, description, and OG tags to match the actual product, and set `metadataBase` so OG images resolve.
2. **Two 404s in global nav** — `/features` and `/pricing` were linked from the header on every page and the homepage CTA, but didn't exist. Built both pages with real content and metadata.
3. **Dead redirect target** — `/faq` permanently redirected to `/#faq`, but no such anchor existed. Added a real FAQ section (with FAQPage JSON-LD) to the homepage.
4. **Broken blog** — the blog index was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, an endpoint that doesn't exist in the repo, so it always failed to load and was invisible to crawlers. Rebuilt it as server-rendered with real content, added dynamic `/blog/[slug]` post pages with per-post metadata.
5. **12 dead-but-indexed URLs** — GSC showed `/blog/post-1`…`/post-12` still carrying ~13k impressions, but those routes don't exist and the topic (email/inbox) has nothing to do with the new product. Added a 301 from the old numeric slugs to `/blog` instead of leaving them 404.
6. **Sitemap stub** — `sitemap.ts` had a TODO that permanently excluded all blog content. Fixed it to enumerate real pages and posts.

Verified with a production build (`npm run build`) and a live server on a clean port — all routes 200, redirects fire correctly, sitemap lists the right URLs. Note: the `http://127.0.0.1:62907` preview didn't respond during this session (connections accepted but hung); this is a shared multi-tenant sandbox, so that's most likely environmental rather than something in the code.

One unrelated item worth flagging: `npm install` reports `next@14.2.5` has a known security vulnerability with a patch available — separate from SEO, but worth a look before shipping.
